title: USING NORTHERN ANALYSIS TO DETECT SRNAS REGULATED BY THE EXOS/CHVI SIGNALING PATHWAY IN SINORHIZOBIUM MELILOTI
author: Mack, Laurena Shane
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Sinorhizobium meliloti  is an alpha-proteobacterium that forms a nitrogen-fixing symbiosis with its plant host  Medicago sativa . This symbiosis is an alternative to manufactured nitrogen fertilizers which contribute to greenhouse gas emissions. The ExoS/ChvI two-component signaling pathway in  S. meliloti  regulates genes that are required for a successful symbiosis. Small regulatory RNAs (sRNAs) are short transcripts that play an important role in regulating gene expression in bacteria. Previous northern blot research aimed to investigate ChvI regulation of 28 sRNA genes using total RNA from cells harvested from rich media. However, 18 sRNAs remained undetected in the previous study. Here, we use RNA enriched for the small RNA fraction and total RNA from cells grown in multiple conditions, including stress conditions, to increase the chances of detecting sRNA gene expression. Also, 44 additional sRNA genes not previously tested by northern analysis are examined in this study. To detect ChvI regulation of sRNA genes, northern analysis was done using RNA extracted from three  S. meliloti  strains ( chvI  partial loss-of-function mutant,  chvI  gain-of-function mutant, and wildtype). An sRNA was determined to be regulated by ChvI if the sRNA showed a reciprocal change in expression in the  chvI  mutants compared to wildtype. Here, we show that three previously undetected sRNA genes are likely be regulated by ChvI and one of the 44 sRNA genes not previously tested by northern analysis is also likely to be regulated by ChvI. We also confirmed that two previously detected sRNA genes are indeed regulated by ChvI.
